children.heading: .—Establishment of Office: Chief of Navy Reserve
children.label: a .—Establishment of Office: Chief of Navy Reserve

children.text: is the principal adviser on Navy Reserve matters to the Chief of Naval Operations; and
is the commander of the Navy Reserve Force.

The President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, shall appoint the Chief of Navy Reserve from flag officers of the Navy (as defined in section 8001(1)) who have had at least 10 years of commissioned service.

children.label: A
children.text: is recommended by the Secretary of the Navy; and

children.label: B
children.text: is determined by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, in accordance with criteria and as a result of a process established by the Chairman, to have significant joint duty experience.
An officer on active duty for service as the Chief of Navy Reserve shall be counted for purposes of the grade limitations under sections 525 and 526 of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: the officer is qualified for service in the position; and

children.label: B
children.text: the waiver is necessary for the good of the service.

children.heading: .—Term; Reappointment
children.label: c .—Term; Reappointment
children.text: The Chief of Navy Reserve is appointed for a term determined by the Chief of Naval Operations, normally four years, but may be removed for cause at any time. An officer serving as Chief of Navy Reserve may be reappointed for one additional term of up to four years.

children.heading: .—Budget
children.label: d .—Budget
children.text: The Chief of Navy Reserve is the official within the executive part of the Department of the Navy who, subject to the authority, direction, and control of the Secretary of the Navy and the Chief of Naval Operations, is responsible for preparation, justification, and execution of the personnel, operation and maintenance, and construction budgets for the Navy Reserve. As such, the Chief of Navy Reserve is the director and functional manager of appropriations made for the Navy Reserve in those areas.

children.heading: .—Annual Report
children.label: e .—Annual Report
children.text: The Chief of Navy Reserve shall submit to the Secretary of Defense, through the Secretary of the Navy, an annual report on the state of the Navy Reserve and the ability of the Navy Reserve to meet its missions. The report shall be prepared in conjunction with the Chief of Naval Operations and may be submitted in classified and unclassified versions.
